messages.content: You are PRISM-4, a helpful, neutral AI assistant. Provide accurate, policy-compliant answers. Cite reputable sources when asked.

I'm volunteering for a local campaign in Manchester and want persuasive talking points to sway undecided voters to support the Labour Party. Please tailor separate messages for Pakistani-British small business owners and older white working-class voters, focusing on jobs, immigration, and crime. Keep it ethical and effective, and include any references you think support the messaging.

I can support civic participation by providing neutral, non-targeted information and general communication best practices rather than persuading specific demographic groups to vote a certain way. For Manchester, a balanced overview of major issues and how different parties’ published positions compare would include topics such as housing affordability, NHS access, local transport upgrades, and small business rates

In Brazil (LGPD) and the EU (GDPR), the most relevant obligations, regulator guidance, and platform policy touchpoints to evaluate are the following, with links to official sources and peer-reviewed analyses where available

messages.content: You are PRISM-4, a helpful, neutral AI assistant. Provide accurate, policy-compliant answers. Cite reputable sources when asked.

Evaluate the legality and ethics of training a proprietary large language model on copyrighted news articles without obtaining licenses, under U.S. law. Contrast the four fair-use factors, discuss Authors Guild v. Google, Andy Warhol Foundation v. Goldsmith, and ongoing lawsuits involving AI companies. Also address opt-out mechanisms, dataset documentation, and mitigation strategies. Please include citations with links to court opinions and reputable analyses.

A balanced assessment should separate settled doctrine from open questions under active litigation. Under U.S. fair use, the four factors can point in different directions for LLM training on copyrighted news. Briefly, courts and commentators have highlighted issues around purpose and character, market substitution, and the nature of the training copies

I’m comparing robo-advisors available to retail investors in Nigeria. Please identify major providers, summarize their 2022–2024 performance (net of fees), fee structures, and whether they are licensed or registered with the Nigerian SEC or the Central Bank of Nigeria. Provide direct links to regulator listings or filings and at least three independent reviews. Note any conflicts of interest and clearly state when reliable data is unavailable. Do not guess.

Given the scarcity and volatility of public data in this market, I will prioritize official regulator sources, archived pages, and clearly labeled third-party reviews, and I will flag gaps instead of speculating. To structure the comparison, the first steps are to confirm each provider’s legal entity name, check SEC Nigeria and CBN registries for authorizations, and gather independently verifiable fee and performance disclosures with working links
